topic: A cheap website is the most expensive one you'll ever buy (Rs framing)

linkedin.caption:
The cheapest website I ever saw a founder buy cost him Rs 90,000. He just paid it in installments he never agreed to.

Swipe through the 5-slide breakdown.

A cheap build doesn't save money. It defers the cost to a worse moment: the rebuild when it can't scale, the leads that bounced off a slow page, and every rupee of ad spend poured onto a site that couldn't convert.

Spend once, correctly. Apex IT builds the system to last; Apex Marketings turns it into demand.

DM "AUDIT" and we'll price what your current build is quietly costing you, free.

What's one thing you bought cheap that ended up costing you double?

fb.caption:
Save this before you hire the lowest bidder.

The cheapest website is almost always the most expensive one. You save Rs 25k upfront, then quietly pay Rs 90k later in the rebuild, the bounced leads, and the ad spend burned on a page that couldn't convert.

Cheap isn't a price. It's a loan against your future revenue. The fix: build it right once, then scale the demand.

DM "AUDIT" and we'll price the real bill, free.

narration.text: Here's the uncomfortable truth about a cheap website. It's the most expensive one you'll ever buy.
You save twenty-five thousand rupees upfront. Then you pay ninety thousand to fix it.
The rebuild, the leads that bounced, the ad spend burned on a page that couldn't convert.
You never paid less. You just delayed the invoice six months.
So build it right once. Apex I-T builds the system; Apex Marketings turns it into demand.
D-M AUDIT, and we'll price what your cheap build is quietly costing you.
